title: Shadows of Brimstone - Part 12 - Blasted Wastes Expansion
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aYgFb-fiwxA

Daniel (@dungeondive) dives into the Blasted Wastes, the most plastic-heavy deluxe expansion for Shadows of Brimstone. This alien prison world delivers two new environments (canyons and wastelands), a Barter Town with unique locations, a day/night cycle, and a small army of wasteland scavengers.

The Barter Town features six randomised locations: a gladiator arena (bet on matches or fight for free), a desert marketplace (alien shoes, rust cycles, scrap wagons), a wasteland workshop (upgradeable scrap armour and junk bomb satchels), a temple with rotating cargo-cult idols (worshipping warheads, krakens, or warp drives), a scavenger dock, and a mining operation. Two new currencies — scrap and tech — drive the economy.

The day/night cycle tracker makes acid pools deadly after dark, while canyon ambushes grant enemies +1 defense on the first round. Five wasteland warbands (Death Claw Raiders, Kraken Born, Night Runners, Sons of Dracoth, Flesh Eaters) modify scavenger encounters. Sand crabs sport a brutal defense of five, and ghost warriors phase in and out of existence. Daniel substitutes dark stone scorpions and hydras into the threat deck to address what he sees as the expansion's one weakness: limited otherworld enemy variety. He compares the whole package to Mad Max and Edgar Rice Burroughs, calling it an amazing value for the sheer volume of content.

----

Which Barter Town location would you visit first — gladiator arena, cargo-cult temple, or wasteland workshop?
